scientific_scope: hypothesis-generating capacity probe, not a jointly trained turn-aware model and not untouched confirmation evidence

motivation.interpretation: Length-controlled complex-junction gradients, frequent downstream rejoin, small suffix gaps, and 15.5 percent excess predicted-left choices justify testing one global non-negative left-turn penalty. They do not establish causality.

turn_model.expanded_state: one state per original directed edge; transition e->f iff head(e)=tail(f)
turn_model.transition_cost: c_f + round(kappa*r) if the e->f planar turn is left, otherwise c_f
turn_model.target: all edge states whose original head is the OD target
turn_model.kappa: median checkpoint edge weight over all graph edges
turn_model.left_definition: signed planar angle > 30 degrees and absolute angle < 150 degrees; positive is counter-clockwise

data.selection_algorithm: rank valid-route indices by SplitMix64(index XOR seed); lowest 20000 are tune and next 10000 are audit
data.confirmation_policy: Do not reuse confirmation A/B. A future positive probe requires a new source-index-disjoint confirmation C.

selection.tie: within 1e-5 choose higher mean edge F1, then smaller r

correctness_gates: at r=0 every sampled OD distance and observed route cost equals the original edge-only CCH result
decoded predicted paths are continuous and connect the declared OD
all expanded arc costs are finite positive u32 values

scientific_gate.required_reporting: point estimates and paired 95 percent percentile intervals for regret, F1, and exact
